description:
Default capability for the Dashboard and Session windows. Grants Tauri's built-in core APIs via `core:default`.

App-local commands registered on this capability are not Tauri-gated in Tauri 2 today (only plugin commands are), but are enumerated here so that a future Tauri upgrade or capability refactor that changes gating semantics produces a reviewable diff against an explicit list:

- provider_status (dashboard only)
- session_list (dashboard only)
- open_session (dashboard only; validates the session id before creating the window — see F-063 / M11)
- session_hello (session-* only)
- session_subscribe (session-* only)
- session_send_message (session-* only)
- session_approve_tool (session-* only)
- session_reject_tool (session-* only)
- get_persistent_approvals (dashboard + session-*; F-036)
- save_approval (dashboard + session-*; F-036)
- remove_approval (dashboard + session-*; F-036)
- terminal_spawn (session-* only; F-125)
- terminal_write (session-* only; F-125, owner-label-bound)
- terminal_resize (session-* only; F-125, owner-label-bound)
- terminal_kill (session-* only; F-125, owner-label-bound)

Per-window authorization is enforced in-process by `require_window_label` (F-051 / H10) or `require_window_label_in` (F-036). Bare identifiers like `allow-open-session` are intentionally omitted from the `permissions` array: Tauri 2's ACL resolver rejects identifiers that do not correspond to a compiled permission file, and Forge does not yet ship app-local permission manifests.
